Ha Long Bay, located in Quang Ninh province, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for its breathtaking natural beauty. It is characterized by emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone islands with tropical forests, creating stunning seascapes. Covering an area of ​​about 1,553 square kilometers, Ha Long Bay is large with more than 1,600 islands and limestone islands. In terms of conservation, Ha Long Bay has a history of millions of years. However it faces challenges such as pollution, overfishing and erosion due to tourism activities and climate change. To preserve Ha Long Bay, there are strict regulations on waste management, limiting the number of tourist boats and promoting sustainable tourism activities.
